L1.area: Archive entry extraction and path processing pipeline

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists due to incomplete Unicode normalization of file paths during archive extraction. On filesystems that are case-insensitive or normalization-insensitive, certain Unicode characters such as ligatures can collide with their multi-character equivalents at the filesystem level, but the library's internal concurrency control system does not recognize these collisions. This allows archive entries with colliding paths to be processed in parallel rather than being serialized, bypassing internal safeguards and enabling symlink poisoning attacks that can lead to arbitrary file overwrites.

L3.area: Archive entry extraction and path processing pipeline
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists due to incomplete Unicode normalization of file paths during archive extraction. On filesystems that are case-insensitive or normalization-insensitive, certain Unicode characters such as ligatures can collide with their multi-character equivalents at the filesystem level, but the library's internal concurrency control system does not recognize these collisions. This allows archive entries with colliding paths to be processed in parallel rather than being serialized, bypassing internal safeguards and enabling symlink poisoning attacks that can lead to arbitrary file overwrites.

ground_truth.reason: The Unicode normalization function uses NFD form which does not collapse ligatures like the sharp-s character into their multi-character equivalents, causing the path reservation system to fail to detect colliding paths on normalization-insensitive filesystems and enabling a TOCTOU race condition.
